definition: Post-1970 crisis recovery duration correlates meaningfully with the demographic backdrop at time of crisis (rank correlation ~0.7-0.8 across 8 crises). Nordics (1991, median age ~37) recovered in 3-5 years; Italy (2008, median age ~43) never recovered to prior peak by 2019. Implies next US recession recovery expected to take 6-10 years vs. 4-6 for GFC.
evidence: 8 post-1970 crises analyzed: rank correlation ~0.7-0.8 between demographic backdrop and recovery time
Italy 2008 (median age ~43): no recovery to prior peak by 2019
Confounded by policy and structural variables but pattern is consistent
open_questions: Can the correlation survive multivariate controls for policy response quality?
Is the relationship linear or does it have threshold effects?
How does AI/automation affect recovery duration for future crises?
